summary: Phase 18 verification. Found CRITICAL bug: Learn Spell flow silently fails to persist. Backend PATCH /api/characters whitelist (routes/characters.py lines 574-581) is missing `cantrips_known`, `spells_known`, `spells_prepared`, so the frontend POSTs these fields and backend responds 400 'No valid fields to update'. Frontend swallows the error (CharacterSheetFull.js handleUpdateCharacter catches & logs only) and shows a misleading 'Learned Fire Bolt' toast while local state updates optimistically — user sees KNOWN badge in the modal but on reload the spell is gone (overview still shows 0 spells). Exhaustion clamping (0-6), temp HP PATCH, edition/ruleset_id POST+GET, multiclass display logic, and Shade rogue template stat fidelity all VERIFIED GOOD.

passed: POST /api/characters with edition=2024 + ruleset_id=dnd5e_2024 -> persisted + returned on GET
GET /api/character-templates/tmpl-shade-rogue -> DEX 15, CON 13, CHA 14 (not default array)
failed: PATCH /api/characters/{id} with {cantrips_known:[...]} -> 400 'No valid fields to update'
PATCH /api/characters/{id} with {spells_known:[...]}  -> 400 'No valid fields to update'
PATCH /api/characters/{id} with {spells_prepared:[...]} -> 400 'No valid fields to update'

passed: learn-spell-btn visible, opens modal with Cantrips/Lvl 1 toggles (learn-level-0/learn-level-1 rendered)
Cantrip list renders with LEARN buttons (learn-add-fire-bolt etc.)
Fire Bolt shows 'KNOWN' badge after (optimistic) learn — badge logic works off local state
Multiclass display logic: CharacterSheetFull lines 597-603 correctly falls back to single-class when class_levels has <=1 entry

Exhaustion display logic: maxHp halved at >=4, speed halved at >=2, speed=0 at >=5 (CharacterSheetFull lines 355-361) — code correct, relies on character.exhaustion_level from backend which IS persisted
failed: Learn Spell persistence end-to-end: toast says 'Learned Fire Bolt' but backend PATCH returned 400, overview still shows '0 SPELLS'. On page reload the spell will NOT be in cantrips_known.

critical.issue: Whitelist at /app/backend/routes/characters.py lines 574-581 is MISSING the fields `cantrips_known`, `spells_known`, `spells_prepared`. These are the exact fields posted by CharacterSpellbook.js learnSpell()/forgetSpell() — so every Learn Spell and Forget Spell call fails with 400 and spells never persist. Add these three keys to the `allowed` set.

ui_bugs.issue: Optimistically updates local state and swallows PATCH errors (console.error only). When the backend rejects the update (e.g. field-not-in-whitelist), the UI appears to succeed and shows a success toast via the caller. This masked the Learn Spell bug. Fix: rethrow the error from the catch block so callers like learnSpell can catch it and show the correct toast.

integration_issues.issue: Frontend PATCH payload `{cantrips_known:[{name:'Fire Bolt',...}]}` is silently dropped by backend whitelist. Needs whitelist fix PLUS handleUpdateCharacter rethrow so future persistence failures surface to user.

action_items: FIX BACKEND: Add 'cantrips_known','spells_known','spells_prepared' to the PATCH whitelist `allowed` set in /app/backend/routes/characters.py around line 574-581.

FIX FRONTEND: In CharacterSheetFull.js handleUpdateCharacter (line 447), rethrow after console.error so callers detect failure and show a real error toast (also roll back the optimistic setCharacter on failure).
RETEST after fix: learn Fire Bolt, reload /characters/{id}, verify overview spell count increments and Fire Bolt appears in Spells tab.
critical_code_review_comments: PATCH whitelist is tightly coupled to the frontend payload shape; whenever the frontend adds a new persistable field (conditions, spells, equipment) the backend whitelist must be updated in lockstep or data is silently lost. Consider either (a) a model-driven partial update (pydantic model_fields intersection) or (b) logging a warning whenever a dropped key is received, to catch these regressions immediately.

handleUpdateCharacter silently swallows errors — dangerous for any optimistic-UI flow (HP, conditions, inspiration, spells). Every caller now has to guess whether the write succeeded. Recommend rethrow + rollback optimistic state in the catch block.
CharacterSpellbook.learnSpell's success toast fires before awaiting confirmation because onUpdateCharacter (handleUpdateCharacter) never throws; the try/catch at line 251-256 is therefore dead code.
Exhaustion and multiclass display logic in CharacterSheetFull (lines 355-361, 597-603) is correct and relies only on persisted backend fields which are clamped correctly on PATCH.
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted TEST_Edition_2024 fighter to verify edition persistence. On Test_Orc_Wiz (a1e7babc-...) PATCHed exhaustion_level and temp HP then reset to 0 so no dirty state remains.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the PATCH whitelist + handleUpdateCharacter rethrow: (1) retest Learn Spell flow incl. reload persistence; (2) then cover the remaining Phase 18 items not re-exercised this run: Full-Builder 9-step Wizard E2E (Half-Elf ASI/language pickers, versatility skills, spell selections, equipment A/B, review submit), Fighter fighting-style gating, Basic builder Wizard submit, Premade AI-match + 'Use Template' for Shade (verify DEX 15 on resulting character), HP/temp HP decrement absorption via UI (temp HP must absorb first then overflow hits current_hit_points) and reload persistence, Conditions Apply Prone -> attack roll shows ▼ -> remove -> ▼ gone, Spell slot consume + Long Rest reset.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: PATCH whitelist in /app/backend/routes/characters.py filters incoming payload keys to a hardcoded `allowed` set; when the Phase 18 Learn Spell feature was added on the frontend (CharacterSpellbook.learnSpell) it sends `cantrips_known`/`spells_known`/`spells_prepared`, but these keys were never added to the backend whitelist. Request is filtered to {} -> 400 'No valid fields to update'. Bug is hidden by handleUpdateCharacter in CharacterSheetFull.js (line 447-454) which does optimistic setCharacter and then catches+logs-only, so the UI shows 'Learned Fire Bolt' success toast even though nothing was persisted. Reproduction: login -> /characters/a1e7babc-c582-48ec-8a64-8c71501fa281 -> Spells tab -> learn-spell-btn -> learn-level-0 -> learn-add-fire-bolt -> Network tab shows PATCH 400 but UI shows success -> reload -> spellbook empty.
overall_status: FAIL — critical Learn Spell persistence bug
